text: I agree with <@UK3LH8CF5>'s criticism as well, and yet I do see a possibility to use licenses as a tool towards a more commons-oriented world. The link is Conway's law. It actually applies in both directions: software architecture both influences and is influenced by the social structure of whatever entity develops the software. A license that pleases commoners and displeases corporations can make a difference. Pleasing includes both legal and moral aspects, plus maybe more. The moral ones probably matter more, given that it's hard to enforce the legal aspects against infringement by big players.
